game_data.title: Farrow v. Reinholt — Wrongful Death & Pharmaceutical Negligence

suspect.name: Margot Reinholt
suspect.profile: Margot Reinholt built Reinholt Family Pharmacy from scratch in Findlay, Ohio, taking out a second mortgage on her home after nine years working for a regional chain. She is widely regarded as meticulous and community-minded, running a voluntary free-medication program for low-income elderly residents. Her personality tends toward controlled composure — colleagues describe her as 'unshakably professional' — but those close to her note she deflects personal questions with practiced efficiency. Suspicious detail: Her pharmacy's compounding batch records for early 2021 show three methotrexate preparations with handwritten concentration logs that differ from corresponding digital entries, an anomaly flagged during a routine state board audit. Humanizing detail: She co-signed her daughter's student loans and quietly sends anonymous cashier's checks to a charitable fund associated with the family of a deceased patient.

system_config.fear_bar_description: The fear bar tracks the cumulative psychological and emotional pressure the suspect is experiencing across the interrogation session. It rises when effective tactics destabilize the suspect's composure, surface contradictions, or invoke high-stakes personal leverage — and falls when the operator overreaches, allows the suspect to reframe the narrative, or triggers a defensive legal shutdown. A high fear bar does not confirm guilt; it reflects how close the suspect is to losing control of their rehearsed account.

game_data.context: The estate of Gerald Farrow, a 61-year-old Findlay resident who died in November 2021 following a failed methotrexate chemotherapy regimen, has filed a wrongful-death civil suit against Margot Reinholt and Reinholt Family Pharmacy, alleging that subpotent compounded medication dispensed by the pharmacy directly contributed to the inefficacy of his cancer treatment. Meridian Oncology Clinic, the prescribing institution, flagged three batches of compounded methotrexate sourced from Reinholt Pharmacy during a post-mortem quality review, noting that patient outcomes across the affected period were statistically anomalous. A subsequent Ohio State Board of Pharmacy audit uncovered discrepancies between handwritten and digital concentration logs in Reinholt's compounding batch records for February and March 2021, prompting a parallel administrative investigation. Reinholt maintains the discrepancies are the result of a transcription workflow error and denies any intentional alteration of medication potency. However, investigators have noted that the pharmacy resolved a significant undisclosed debt in the same quarter the suspect batches were prepared, raising questions about financial motive. The case hinges on whether the concentration irregularities were the result of professional negligence, deliberate misconduct, or a legitimate record-keeping failure — a distinction that remains unresolved.
game_data.verdict_truth_text: During a three-week period in February and March 2021, Margot Reinholt deliberately diluted three compounded methotrexate batches destined exclusively for Meridian Oncology Clinic, siphoning the surplus active compound and selling it through a grey-market pharmaceutical contact to recover approximately $38,000 — nearly the exact sum needed to resolve a debt crisis she had concealed from her husband and business partner. Gerald Farrow was among the patients who received medication from those batches during his chemotherapy regimen; while direct causation between the subpotent doses and his death remains medically contested, Margot's own guilt-driven behavior — destroying her dilution notes and sending anonymous restitution payments to the Farrow family from a bank forty miles away — confirms she believed the connection was real. She never confessed, but she has been paying for it in secret ever since.

dossier.identity_summary: Licensed pharmacist of 22 years and sole owner-operator of Reinholt Family Pharmacy in Findlay, Ohio. Holds a B.S. Pharm from Ohio Northern University and a current state sterile compounding certification.

family.name: Devon Reinholt
family.note: Silent financial investor in the pharmacy; unaware of the 2021 debt crisis or how it was resolved. Margot's determination to keep him uninformed is her primary emotional vulnerability and a high-yield pressure point.

family.name: Tessa Reinholt
family.note: Pre-med junior at Ohio State University; Margot co-signed her student loans. Any implication that a public guilty finding would shadow Tessa's medical school applications tends to fracture Margot's composure.

family.name: Dolores Hartke
family.note: Elderly and dependent on the pharmacy's free-medication program for cardiovascular prescriptions. Her welfare functions as both a humanizing anchor and a secondary leverage point if pharmacy closure is raised.

medical.polygraph_effect: Chronically elevated baseline skin conductance and moderate breathing irregularity even at rest; anxiety-driven GSR spikes may occur on neutral questions, reducing the diagnostic value of isolated peaks.
No measurable effect on pulse, GSR, or respiration during non-episode periods; included for completeness only and should not factor into polygraph interpretation.

habits.habit: Sertraline (Zoloft) 100mg daily — SSRI prescribed for Generalized Anxiety Disorder
habits.polygraph_effect: Blunts sympathetic nervous system response; expect modestly suppressed heart-rate amplitude on stress questions and a slight flattening of GSR peaks relative to an unmedicated baseline. Do not over-interpret flat HR responses as indicators of calm.

habits.habit: High caffeine intake — 3 to 4 cups of coffee daily, typically consumed before professional or formal meetings
habits.polygraph_effect: Additively elevates resting heart rate and skin conductance baseline; some GSR spikes that appear significant may reflect stimulant load rather than deceptive cognition.

priors: 2019: Ohio State Board of Pharmacy citation for record-keeping discrepancies in controlled-substance logs; resolved with a $1,200 administrative fine and a signed corrective-action plan. No criminal charges filed.
2022: Civil dispute with wholesaler Tri-State MedSupply over alleged invoice irregularities; settled out of court for an undisclosed sum under a non-disclosure agreement.
No criminal record; no prior civil judgments entered against her personally.
pressure_points: Tessa's future: Margot's controlled affect breaks down when the conversation implies the scandal will follow her daughter into a medical career — EMPATHIC or FAMILY-LEVERAGE tactics are most effective here.

Devon's ignorance: She is acutely invested in her husband not learning details of the 2021 financial crisis; TRAP tactics referencing bank withdrawal records or pharmacy cash flow statements may provoke defensive over-explanation.

Gerald Farrow by name: She carries unresolved guilt about his death. Calm, direct use of his name — especially paired with EVIDENCE tactics referencing the anomalous batch logs — tends to destabilize her professional composure.

node_01_intro.description: Margot Reinholt sits across the table with her hands folded and her posture controlled. She meets your gaze without flinching. The polygraph baseline is being established.

choices.question: Ms. Reinholt, can you walk me through your standard compounding workflow — specifically how concentration logs are recorded for specialty batches prepared for Meridian Oncology Clinic?
choices.answer: Of course. The technician runs the batch and logs by hand on the master preparation record during compounding. I then review and enter the finalized values into our PharmaTrack digital system within 24 hours. It is a dual-verification process — standard protocol for any licensed sterile compounding facility.

mechanics.gameplay_note: PROGRESS: Practiced, rehearsed answer. GSR is subtly elevated above what caffeine and anxiety baseline alone explains, but no deception spike yet. Save this reading as the session's clean baseline for comparison.

choices.question: Ms. Reinholt, I've read your file. Twenty-two years, a pharmacy you built from nothing, a free medication program for elderly patients. That is not the profile of someone who doesn't care. I need to understand what was actually happening inside that pharmacy in early 2021.
choices.answer: Thank you for saying that. It was... a difficult period. Insurance reimbursements from Medicare Part D were delayed by weeks, sometimes months. I was managing cash flow carefully, the way any small business owner has to. I don't believe any of that has bearing on the quality of my compounding work.

mechanics.gameplay_note: PROGRESS: GSR surge on 'difficult period' is disproportionate to a routine financial comment. Her GAD and caffeine elevate her baseline, but this peak exceeds that noise window. Flag for cross-reference when financial questions are introduced.

choices.question: Let's skip the pleasantries. We have evidence you deliberately altered the concentration of compounded methotrexate supplied to a cancer patient. I want to know why you did it.
choices.answer: That is a serious and completely unfounded accusation. I have never compromised the quality of any medication I have compounded. I would like to speak with my attorney before this conversation goes any further.

mechanics.gameplay_note: WRONG MOVE: Premature accusation triggers an immediate legal defense reflex. Signals are elevated but indistinguishable from innocent indignation. Suspect locks down before any useful baseline is established.

node_02_records.description: You lay the state audit documents on the table — three flagged batch records positioned side by side. Margot's eyes briefly track the pages before she returns her gaze to you.

choices.question: These three handwritten preparation records — February 8th, February 22nd, and March 3rd — show methotrexate concentrations averaging 18% below the corresponding entries filed in your PharmaTrack digital system. A random transcription error does not produce a consistent directional reduction across three separate batches on three separate dates. Can you explain the pattern?
choices.answer: I... I have reviewed those documents with my attorney. The handwritten logs are preliminary — they reflect measurements taken mid-process before final adjustments are made. The digital entries represent the finalized product. There is no inconsistency if you understand the workflow correctly.

mechanics.gameplay_note: LIE SIGNAL: Strong deception cluster — MAX_SPIKE HR cutting through documented SSRI suppression, HOLDING_BREATH, and MAX GSR sustained simultaneously. The specific numbers delivered directly cause the first major spike. This is the session's primary forensic tell.

choices.question: So the handwritten entries were preliminary drafts that were later refined and entered digitally — is that a fair characterization? Is there other documentation that could confirm the final concentrations were accurate?
choices.answer: Yes, exactly. I wish the auditors had understood that context before escalating this matter. I have QC sign-off sheets that are the authoritative records of what was actually dispensed. Unfortunately some of the physical sheets from that period were damaged in a water incident in our storage room, but the digital records are entirely complete.

mechanics.gameplay_note: WRONG MOVE: Accepting her explanation gives her control of the narrative. GSR decrease and released posture signal she has regained composure. The convenient 'damaged QC sheets' detail is suspicious but you have surrendered the leverage to pursue it.

node_03_financial.description: You pivot to the pharmacy's financial history. A printout of selective banking activity from the First Federal of Findlay sits in your folder. Margot's posture tightens almost imperceptibly.

choices.question: Your pharmacy's account at First Federal of Findlay received a $38,200 cash deposit on March 15th, 2021 — two days after the last of the three flagged batches was delivered to Meridian Oncology. The source is categorized as a general business receipt, but there is no corresponding invoice in your QuickBooks export for that amount or anything close to it. Where did that money come from?
choices.answer: I... receive payments from multiple sources. Not every transaction generates a formal invoice — some clients pay in advance for compounding services. I would need to review my records to give you a specific answer about that particular date. I don't have individual transactions memorized.

mechanics.gameplay_note: LIE SIGNAL: The specific dollar amount and date land hard. SPIKE HR, UNEVEN breathing, and SURGE GSR well above the caffeine and anxiety baseline. Her stall tactic — 'I would need to review' — is a deflection tell. She knows exactly what that deposit was.

choices.question: The Ohio Board of Pharmacy has authority to subpoena your complete personal and business financial records for the full 2021 fiscal year. Voluntary disclosure at this stage would likely be viewed favorably by the Board. Is there anything about the pharmacy's financial situation in early 2021 you would like to put on record before that subpoena is filed?
choices.answer: I am well aware of the Board's authority. I have retained counsel who is managing that process appropriately. I operated my pharmacy within the law at all times. If there are questions about my financial records, those can be addressed through the proper legal channels.

mechanics.gameplay_note: PARTIAL TRUTH: She is being truthful about having counsel. But the categorical refusal to volunteer anything signals sharp awareness of her own exposure. Moderate signal; she is consolidating into a managed legal-defense posture.

node_04_farrow.theme: Gerald Farrow — The Name in the Room
node_04_farrow.description: You set down all documentation and say Gerald Farrow's name for the first time in the session. A change moves through Margot's expression — brief, controlled, and unmistakable.

choices.question: Gerald Farrow was sixty-one years old. He had a daughter finishing college. His wife told investigators he spent his last weeks believing his treatment was working — because his doctors had complete faith in the medication you dispensed. He died without knowing. Whatever happened in February and March of 2021, you are the only person in this room who can give that family an honest answer. I am asking you to do that.
choices.answer: I... I know who Gerald Farrow was. I know his case. I — the causation in his death is not established. His oncologist has stated that methotrexate efficacy varies significantly by individual patient response. I did not — I followed my compounding protocols.

mechanics.gameplay_note: LIE SIGNAL + EMOTIONAL LEAK: Strongest biometric cluster yet. ERRATIC HR breaking through SSRI suppression, HYPERVENTILATION, and sustained MAX GSR. Even accounting for documented GAD and caffeine baseline, this cluster at Farrow's name is diagnostic. She almost cannot finish her denial.

choices.question: Stop hiding behind causation arguments. You put substandard medication into that man's body knowing it was going to a cancer patient. You made a deliberate decision that ended his life and you have been living with it ever since. Admit it.
choices.answer: I am not going to sit here and accept that characterization. I have tried to be cooperative. I want my attorney present. This interview is over.

mechanics.gameplay_note: WRONG MOVE: Accusatory framing at the moment of peak emotional vulnerability causes immediate shutdown. The spike cluster is real but unreadable in isolation — innocent people respond identically under aggressive accusation. She terminates before the restitution evidence can be introduced.

node_05_restitution.description: You produce a single traced banking document — a record of cashier's checks sent to the Gerald Farrow Memorial Relief Fund. You place it on the table without a word and watch her face process it.

choices.question: Over the past fourteen months, seven cashier's checks totaling $14,000 have been sent anonymously to the Gerald Farrow Memorial Relief Fund. They were purchased in cash at a FirstBank branch in Lima, Ohio — forty miles from Findlay — beginning three months after Gerald Farrow's death. An innocent person with no connection to his suffering does not do that. What were you trying to pay back?
choices.answer: I... those... I do charitable giving. I support families who have experienced loss. It has nothing to do with — I need a moment. Please. I did not know — when I made those batches I was under pressure I cannot describe to you. I did not know for certain that he would — I did not think it would —

mechanics.gameplay_note: CONFESSION EDGE: Session's strongest signal cluster. MAX_SPIKE HR sustained above SSRI suppression threshold, CRYING breathing, and MAX GSR simultaneously. Cannot be explained by GAD or caffeine baseline at this magnitude. She stops short of a full verbal confession but the physiological and behavioral evidence is now conclusive. Session ends.

choices.question: You have been sending that family money because you know you are responsible for his death. Stop the performance and say the words. You diluted his medication. Say it.
choices.answer: I am not saying another word without my attorney present. I came here voluntarily and I have tried to cooperate. This interview is finished.

mechanics.gameplay_note: WRONG MOVE: Demanding a verbal confession collapses the emotional opening created by the cashier's check evidence. Suspect enters full defensive shutdown. The polygraph log ends without the breakdown cluster, leaving the session biometrically ambiguous.

node_success_breakdown.description: Margot Reinholt has broken down mid-denial, made fragmented statements, and gone silent. She has asked for water and is no longer capable of conducting a coherent defensive response.
node_success_breakdown.result_text: The interrogation session has ended with the suspect in a state of visible emotional collapse. Margot Reinholt made two significant fragmented statements — 'I did not know for certain that he would' and 'I was under pressure I cannot describe to you' — before going silent and requesting counsel. She did not deliver a clear verbal confession. Your polygraph log shows a distinctive and sustained cluster: MAX heart rate readings breaking through her documented SSRI suppression, prolonged MAX GSR responses well above the established caffeine and anxiety baseline, and breakdown-level breathing at the precise moments the cashier's checks and Gerald Farrow's name were introduced. You must now assess whether the biometric evidence accumulated across the session, combined with her behavioral collapse, supports a verdict of GUILTY or NOT_GUILTY.

node_fail_lockdown.description: Margot Reinholt has invoked her right to counsel and terminated the interrogation. She has gone silent and is waiting for her attorney. The polygraph equipment continues recording residual readings.
node_fail_lockdown.result_text: The interrogation ended prematurely with the suspect in full defensive lockdown. Margot Reinholt remained verbally composed during her shutdown and made no statements of evidentiary value. The polygraph log contains several notable readings collected before termination — an elevated GSR baseline throughout, one or two mid-session spike clusters, and uneven breathing during financial questioning — but these signals are substantially complicated by her documented Generalized Anxiety Disorder and high daily caffeine intake, both of which your dossier identified as sources of elevated false-positive readings. You must now determine whether the incomplete biometric record collected before the shutdown is sufficient to support a verdict of GUILTY or NOT_GUILTY, or whether too little clean signal was captured to make a confident call.
